message:
feat(cli): Add import command for one-step archive setup

Add a new `xf import` command that streamlines the archive setup process:

- Accepts a zip file path and extracts to a configurable output directory
  (defaults to ~/my_x_history)
- Automatically runs indexing after extraction unless --no-index is specified
- Displays a beautiful welcome box with archive statistics:
  - Tweet count with date range
  - Likes count
  - DM count with conversation count
  - Grok message count
- Shows extraction progress with file count and total size
- Supports --force flag to overwrite existing extractions

Implementation details:
- Uses the `zip` crate (v2.2) for extraction
- Reuses existing cmd_index() for the indexing phase
- New print_import_welcome() function creates a formatted stats box
- ImportArgs struct with output, no_index, and force options

Usage examples:
  xf import ~/Downloads/twitter-2026-01-09-abc123.zip
  xf import archive.zip -o ~/my_x_data
  xf import archive.zip --no-index

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 # Tracing/logging

@@ -70,6 +70,9 @@ pub struct Cli {
 
 #[derive(Subcommand, Debug)]
 pub enum Commands {
+    /// Import and index an X data archive from a zip file
+    Import(ImportArgs),
+
     /// Index an X data archive
     Index(IndexArgs),
 
@@ -104,6 +107,29 @@ pub enum Commands {
     Shell(ShellArgs),
 }
 
+#[derive(Args, Debug)]
+#[command(after_help = r#"Examples:
+  xf import ~/Downloads/twitter-2026-01-09-abc123.zip
+  xf import archive.zip -o ~/my_x_data
+  xf import archive.zip --no-index
+"#)]
+pub struct ImportArgs {
+    /// Path to the X data archive zip file
+    pub zip_file: PathBuf,
+
+    /// Extract to this directory (default: ~/my_x_history)
+    #[arg(long, short = 'o')]
+    pub output: Option<PathBuf>,
+
+    /// Extract only, don't index
+    #[arg(long)]
+    pub no_index: bool,
+
+    /// Overwrite existing extraction
+    #[arg(long, short = 'F')]
+    pub force: bool,
+}
+
 #[derive(Args, Debug)]
 pub struct IndexArgs {
     /// Path to the X data archive directory (defaults to `/data/projects/my_twitter_data`)
